methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $35M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
QuamCore is a Series A quantum computing company founded by Alon Cohen and Yonatan Cohen, having raised $35M to date. The valuation universe comprises 11 peers across quantum computing: 3 public companies (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum) and 8 private firms including IonQ, Quantinuum, and Infleqtion.

Stage-progression analysis is applicable here, using the $35M total raised as a proxy for implied Series A valuation and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage step-ups, yielding $70M. Peer-median comparison is not applicable due to insufficient comparable datapoints; while 7 same-sector peers exist, the narrow filters for same-stage, adjacent-stage, and similar-tier companies produced fewer than the 3 minimum required comparables, making a median-based approach misleading rather than informative.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $60M to $80M, with a median of $70M. This range rests on a single applicable method, which is the primary limitation of the analysis. Key uncertainties include the absence of sufficient private quantum-computing peers at Series A stage and the reliance on stage-progression mechanics rather than direct market comparables. Any forward valuation should be stress-tested against technology development milestones and capital efficiency relative to the broader quantum-computing sector.
